IG holds the edge from their Week 2 2-0 sweep of WE, bolstered by veterans Rookie in mid and Meiko on support, despite a 0-2 loss to Anyone's Legend last week following a 2-1 reverse over Weibo Gaming. Stable rosters on both sides amid Patch 26.7 meta emphasize IG's macro execution and head-to-head advantage in the double round-robin format, where WE fights relegation pressure while IG eyes playoffs.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Despite Nicolás Maduro's January 3, 2026, capture by U.S. forces and extradition to face charges, trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 62.9% probability he resumes as Venezuela's leader by December 31, reflecting doubts on the durability of his ouster amid entrenched chavista institutions, military loyalty, and pro-regime narratives framing it as a kidnapping. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez, Maduro's vice president sworn in January 5, commands 23.5% as she consolidates power past the 90-day constitutional limit, evidenced by recent U.S. oil, gas, and mining deals signaling normalization without elections. Opposition leader María Corina Machado trails at 8%, backed by a unified coalition demanding swift presidential vote upon her planned year-end return, while UN assessments highlight persistent repressive state apparatus and stalled democratic transition.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 165-189 tweets from May 4 to May 6, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 4 12:00 PM ET to May 6, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: NV-01 House Election Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. Recent April 29 reporting highlights intra-party criticism of Titus's Laken Riley Act vote amid calls for generational change, but her historical general election margins (52% in 2024) and weak challenger resources minimize primary risks ahead of June 9. A fragmented Republican primary field, including state Sen. Carrie Buck and others, limits GOP viability per Likely Democratic ratings from Cook and Sabato's Crystal Ball.

markets.question: Will Moldova be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: With first rehearsals underway at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle ahead of the May 12 and 14 semi-finals and May 16 grand final, early impressions from staging photos and clips have solidified Finland's frontrunner status in trader consensus for a top 3 finish, thanks to Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's high-energy "Liekinheitin"—a UMK standout blending violin flair and pop hooks that overtook Israel in betting markets post-national selections. Greece, Denmark, France, and Israel also draw strong support from robust entries and historical televote strength, while jury-friendly narratives favor Nordic campaigns. All 35 acts are locked in after recent national finals like Poland's and Romania's; watch for jury rehearsals and semi draw impacts on momentum.

markets.question: Will Stade Brestois 29 win on 2026-05-13?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 13, 2026
If Stade Brestois 29 wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Stade Brestois 29 vs. RC Strasbourg Alsace
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for Wednesday, May 13, 2026 between Stade Brestois 29 and RC Strasbourg Alsace.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Brest victory at 46% implied probability ahead of their Ligue 1 home clash against Strasbourg, reflecting strong home form at Stade Francis-Le Blé and an unbeaten record in the last two head-to-heads—a 2-1 away win in November 2025 and a 0-0 draw earlier that year. Strasbourg, sitting 8th with 46 points from 30 matches, holds competitive 34% odds buoyed by an unbeaten run in five games including recent away wins, but faces mounting injury concerns with Aaron Anselmino (hamstring), Valentin Barco (ankle), Guéla Doué (virus), and others sidelined, thinning their squad depth. Brest (11th, 38 points) contends with Bradley Locko out (hamstring) and suspensions, yet the matchup projects as tightly contested with a 33% draw chance amid mid-table stakes.

markets.question: Will JD Gaming qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

Stroud's ascent, and 2025 division winners like the 14-3 Patriots and Broncos add upset potential, keeping the top cluster tight amid favorable schedules and roster health for multiple contenders.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 22°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated May 3, implies a minimum temperature of 21-22°C on May 5 under mainly cloudy skies with east force 4-5 winds and occasional showers, driven by a departing northeast monsoon bringing cooler air to Guangdong after a recent cold front. Trader consensus reflects this tight forecast range, with 21°C and 22°C edging ahead at 41% amid uncertainty from variable cloud cover limiting nocturnal radiative cooling and shower timing potentially enhancing evaporative cooling for lower mins around 20°C or allowing brighter intervals to push toward 23°C. Climatological May lows average 23°C, but current upper-air disturbances add volatility; watch the 16:30 HKT update for refined model runs.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in the closely contested 25–39.9% range, with 30.0–34.9% leading at 34.8% implied probability, reflecting March 2026 INDEC data showing 3.4% monthly CPI—the year's highest—lifting year-to-date cumulative inflation to 9.4% and annual rate to 32.6% year-over-year from February's 33.2%. This uptick, driven by transportation and education costs amid regulated price adjustments under President Milei's austerity, has tempered optimism despite fiscal surpluses and monetary tightening; Reuters' median economist forecast holds at 30.0% average for 2026, matching IMF's revised 30.5% projection. Key swing factors include April CPI release around May 14 and adherence to zero-deficit targets, with any renewed monthly acceleration above 3% potentially favoring higher bins.

markets.question: Theo FDV above $1B one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Theo's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Theo (https://x.com/Theo_Network) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Apex at a 99.1% implied probability to claim the top spot on Netflix's US movies Top 10 this week, driven by its explosive debut since the April 24 release. The Charlize Theron and Taron Egerton-led survival thriller amassed 38.2 million global views in its first full week—more than double runner-up 180's 17.5 million—fueled by strong critical reception praising its tense Australian wilderness action and star power. It currently holds the #1 position on Netflix's US movies chart, outpacing holdovers like Roommates and Thrash. An upset would require an unprecedented late-week surge from a new entrant like Swapped, but Apex's entrenched momentum and proximity to chart lock make that a remote scenario.

markets.question: WTT - Men's Singles: Austria vs India
markets.description:
This market refers to the table tennis match between Austria and India in a WTT event, scheduled for May 4 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Austria' if Austria wins against India.

This market will resolve to 'India' if India wins against Austria.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will StepFun have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank among primarily Chinese companies, based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Qualifying Chinese Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Meituan” would be ranked ahead of “Xiaomi”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This late-April surge explains Baidu's 45% trader consensus lead, reflecting the market's skin-in-the-game assessment of its current dominance ahead of the May 31 snapshot. markets.description:

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no U.S. acquisition of Greenland territory in 2026, driven by firm rejections from Denmark and Greenland officials affirming the island's sovereignty is not for sale, alongside legal barriers under international law prohibiting forcible annexation. Early-year Republican bills like the Greenland Annexation and Statehood Act authorized presidential negotiations but stalled amid bipartisan opposition and UN warnings against coercive tactics. Trump's tariff threats on NATO allies opposing the push backfired, escalating diplomatic tensions without yielding talks. Recent Pentagon discussions focus on expanded military access at Pituffik Space Base rather than ownership, underscoring strategic cooperation over territorial control amid Arctic security concerns. Absent bilateral agreements by year-end, structural hurdles persist.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MT-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MT-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Paris be 11°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's May 2 announcement of withdrawing 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months—and hints of far larger cuts—has intensified transatlantic friction amid allies' limited support for U.S. operations in the Iran conflict, prompting NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte to state on May 4 that Europeans have "gotten the message" and are stepping up. However, a full U.S. withdrawal from NATO remains improbable due to 2023 legislation mandating 120-day congressional notice and approval, facing bipartisan resistance from lawmakers concerned about weakening the alliance against threats like Russia and China. Trader consensus reflects this, pricing year-end exit odds below 10%, with institutional hurdles and historical precedent favoring continuity despite ongoing diplomatic pressures.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 15°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This aligns trader consensus around mid-teens outcomes, with 17°C or higher at 45% implied probability reflecting model ensemble spread (some runs up to 17°C if precip delays) and potential urban heat island boost in city-center readings versus cooler airport data at Pearson. May 3's observed high of 14°C amid clouds underscores spring variability near seasonal normals of 17°C max; new guidance expected this afternoon could shift odds as shower timing impacts peak heating.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Apologize" or "Apology" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 1160-1199 tweets in May 2026?

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

markets.question: T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final: Philippines vs Indonesia - Toss Match Double Philippines Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Philippines and Indonesia scheduled for 2026-05-07 in T20 World Cup, East Asia-Pacific Qualifier, Regional Final.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Philippines will be considered correct if  Philippines is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Indonesia will be considered correct if Indonesia is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone HICP annual inflation surged to a flash 3.0% in April 2026—the highest since September 2023 and up from 2.6% in March—driving Polymarket trader consensus to imply 42.5% odds for the 2026 full-year average at 3.1% or higher, followed by 27.2% for 2.8-3.0%. This momentum stems from energy prices rocketing to +10.9%, fueled by Middle East geopolitical tensions, while core inflation held steady at 2.2%. ECB staff March projections lifted the 2026 HICP forecast to 2.6% on elevated oil and gas wholesale costs, yet recent data signals upside risks amid sticky services inflation near 3.0% and softening Q1 GDP growth. Watch May flash HICP and the June ECB policy meeting for potential shifts.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Singapore be 25°C on May 5?

eventMetadata.context_description: NEA's latest fortnightly outlook for 30 April–14 May 2026 forecasts inter-monsoon conditions over Singapore with light, variable winds and thundery showers on most afternoons, projecting daily maximum temperatures of 33–35°C, occasionally exceeding 35°C. This drives trader consensus toward 32°C (46% implied probability) as the leading outcome for May 5, with 33°C close at 30.5%, as convective showers frequently cap peak heating despite strong solar insolation and urban heat island effects boosting recent highs to 34.5°C on May 4 at Clementi. Climatological May averages hover around 31°C, but ENSO-neutral conditions transitioning to El Niño support warmth; watch NEA's next 24-hour update and station observations for refinements amid model uncertainty in shower coverage.

markets.question: Will The Deep die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
markets.description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

This positioning stems from a significant temperature drop observed midday on May 4—becoming unseasonably low for early May per Israel Meteorological Service guidance—due to cooler Mediterranean air advection and mid-to-high-level cloud cover suppressing solar heating. Below the typical May average of 25–26°C, these conditions limit intensification potential, though low precipitation odds (9–25%) introduce minor uncertainty; official observations from Ben Gurion Airport will resolve the market as models update overnight.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Shevchenko by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Shevchenko, Donetsk Oblast, (48.384157880601364° N, 37.10809761679785° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue probing Ukrainian defenses northwest of Pokrovsk in Donetsk Oblast, launching assaults near Hryshyne and toward Shevchenko, Serhiivka, and Novooleksandrivka, as detailed in Institute for the Study of War assessments from early to mid-April 2026. Ukrainian Air Assault Forces recently reported repelling Russian pushes along northern Pokrovsk outskirts and striking over 30 vehicles on the Shevchenkove-Pokrovsk logistics route with HIMARS, thwarting reinforcements as of late April. No geolocated footage confirms Russian entry into Shevchenko per frontline maps like DeepStateUA, sustaining a contested stalemate despite intensified pressure. Upcoming frontline developments, including potential escalations or Ukrainian counterstrikes, will shape resolution based on ISW territorial updates.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 72.5% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, driven by unmatched depth across 16 UEFA qualifiers including top-ranked powerhouses Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, Netherlands, and Croatia—all advancing comfortably through group winners and playoffs without major shocks. South America's 21% reflects defending champions Argentina, alongside Brazil, Uruguay, Colombia, Ecuador, and Paraguay, who secured their six CONMEBOL slots amid tight qualifying battles culminating in March wins like Argentina's clincher over Bolivia. Recent inter-confederation playoffs in late March confirmed underdogs like Iraq (AFC), Congo DR (CAF), and Curaçao (CONCACAF), but failed to elevate Asia (3.1%), Africa (2.9%), North America (2.5%), or Oceania (0.1%) contenders, preserving Europe's dominance ahead of the June 11 group stage kickoff.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 75.5% implied probability against another U.S. sovereign debt downgrade before 2027, reflecting rating agencies' stable outlooks amid escalating fiscal pressures. Fitch Ratings reaffirmed its 'AA+'/Stable rating on April 30, 2026, citing widening deficits and debt held by the public surpassing 100% of GDP—reaching $31.27 trillion versus $31.22 trillion nominal GDP as of March—yet balanced by resilient growth and the dollar's reserve status. No further actions have followed Moody's May 2025 cut from Aaa to Aa1, with S&P at AA+/Stable. Key catalysts include potential debt ceiling negotiations and FY2027 budget debates, alongside CBO projections of $1.9 trillion annual deficits.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Joe Wilson's strong fundraising lead—over $669,000 raised with $294,000 cash on hand as of late March—and the district's R+7 partisan lean position the Republican Party as heavy 82.5% trader favorite to retain SC-02, consistent with Cook Political's Solid Republican rating and Trump's 14-point 2024 win here. Democrats face steep odds at 18%, with no House victory in the district since 1965 amid a fragmented primary field including Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief. Near-even pricing on A (49.5%), B (50%), and Other (50.5%) reflects pre-primary uncertainty ahead of the June 9 Republican contest against Wilson's challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond, whom traders view as longshots given his incumbency and past 59%+ general election margins. No major developments have emerged in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 48% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated June 2026 IPO—potentially the largest ever at a $1.75 trillion valuation—owing to its deep ties with Elon Musk, including underwriting Tesla's 2010 debut and the recent return of veteran banker Michael Grimes to head investment banking. A April 6 virtual meeting with 21 syndicate banks, led by Musk and CFO Bret Johnsen, reaffirmed Morgan Stanley alongside Bank of America (19.4%) and Goldman Sachs (18.5%) in senior bookrunning roles, amid plans for a $75 billion raise and unprecedented 30% retail allocation. With prospectus expected late May and roadshow starting June 8, shared "lead left" duties could dilute positioning, but SpaceX's Starship milestones and Starlink expansion underpin the high-stakes tech financing race.

events.title: UFC Fight Night: Bernardo Sopaj vs. Timmy Cuamba (Bantamweight, Main Card)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Bernardo Sopaj" if Bernardo Sopaj is officially declared the winner of the fight against Timmy Cuamba at UFC Fight Night: Allen vs. Costa, scheduled for May 16, 2026.

It will resolve to "Timmy Cuamba" if Timmy Cuamba is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 30, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: In this closely contested bantamweight bout on the UFC Vegas 117 main card, Timmy Cuamba (10-3, UFC 2-2) carries momentum from back-to-back wins, including a flying knee KO of Roberto Romero in April 2025 and unanimous decision over ChangHo Lee in November 2025, leveraging his 5'9" frame, 71" reach, and striker style (2.77 SLpM). Bernardo Sopaj (12-3, UFC 1-1), a high-volume orthodox striker (4.24 SLpM, 56% accuracy) with solid wrestling (2.53 TD avg.), returns after a 16-month layoff following his January 2025 unanimous decision rebound over Ricky Turcios, post-KO loss to Vinicius Oliveira. No recent injuries or cancellations reported ahead of May 16 at the APEX; stylistic clash favors Sopaj's pressure against Cuamba's distance management.

markets.question: Will Marta Kostyuk win the 2026 Women’s French Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka as the clear 2026 Women's French Open favorite at 31.5% implied probability, driven by her dominant 25-1 record this season and straight-sets clay-court wins over Peyton Stearns, Jaqueline Cristian, and Naomi Osaka in Madrid, signaling improved red dirt adaptation. Iga Świątek's odds have slipped to 16.5% following her illness-forced retirement in Madrid's second round against Ann Li last week, amid a tough clay swing that raises fitness concerns for the four-time champion. Elena Rybakina's Stuttgart title over Karolina Muchová elevates her to 13.3%, while defending champion Coco Gauff sits at 9% after a round-of-16 Madrid exit to Linda Nosková; teen sensation Mirra Andreeva's Madrid final run justifies 7.5%, with Marta Kostyuk's tournament win at 4.4%.

markets.question: Will Mohamed Salah play in Scottish Premiership next?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the league of next club that Mohamed Salah officially joins by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET.

If Mohamed Salah does not officially join a new club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah signs a contract that includes a delayed transfer or loan-back clause requiring him to remain at Liverpool for a specified period before joining another club, this market will resolve to the club he is officially contracted to join following that period.

If Mohamed Salah joins a club in a league that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

Transfers or assignments between a club’s senior team and reserve or affiliate teams will not be considered for this market.

An official transfer announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from Liverpool, Mohamed Salah and/or the acquiring club. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

Recent Middle East tensions have introduced uncertainty, elevating MLS to 28% amid Inter Miami's concrete interest to pair him with Messi, while Egyptian Premier League pricing at 32.9% captures national sentiment for a homecoming with Al Ahly or Zamalek as Egypt captain. Fresh reports of Fenerbahce's secret agent meetings have lifted Süper Lig to 10.4%, and Primeira Liga (17%) reflects sporadic Benfica/Porto links, though top European leagues trail given his age and free-agent status.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 93% for direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, reflecting Pyongyang's unresponsiveness amid deepening military ties with Russia, including a planned 2027-2031 cooperation pact discussed during Defense Minister Andrei Belousov's late-April visit to Kim Jong Un. Despite South Korean President Lee Jae-myung's repeated March calls for dialogue and a rare North Korean gesture of praise for Seoul's apology over a drone incursion earlier in April, no high-level meetings have been scheduled, and North Korea has prioritized ambassadorial appointments to Global South nations over inter-Korean engagement. With under two months remaining and historical deadlock persisting absent a sudden diplomatic breakthrough, traders see formidable barriers to direct bilateral negotiations.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in New York City be between 82-83°F on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service guidance for the New York City area projects highs near LaGuardia Airport (KLGA) in the upper 77s to low 80s on May 5, fueled by a building upper-level ridge over the Northeast and warm southerly flow at 850 hPa levels (11-13°C), well above the early May climatological average of 70°F. Trader consensus narrowly favors 78-79°F (34%) over 80-81°F (31.5%), with the split stemming from ensemble model discrepancies—GFS runs trending slightly warmer inland while ECMWF emphasizes coastal sea breeze moderation that could cap peaks 2-4°F lower in the afternoon. Minimal cloud cover and low precipitation risk support these elevated temps, but new 12Z GFS/ECMWF updates this afternoon may sharpen the outlook before resolution.

markets.question: Fed rate cut by December 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for December 2026, currently scheduled for December 8-9. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no December meeting takes place by January 7, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate cut by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for January 2026, currently scheduled for January 27-28. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no January meeting takes place by February 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 29, 2026 meeting—the third consecutive hold—citing elevated inflation risks after March Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 3.3% year-over-year, exceeding the 2% target, alongside resilient labor market conditions. This stance aligns with market-implied odds from CME FedWatch Tool showing over 96% probability of no change at the June 16-17 FOMC gathering, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket that near-term rate cuts remain unlikely amid sticky price pressures and balanced growth. Traders should monitor April CPI release on May 12 for potential shifts in easing expectations, with Treasury yields stable around recent levels signaling sustained policy patience.

markets.question: Will Claudia López win the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Colombia presidential election market prices unidentified moderate Candidate M a narrow 49.5% favorite for the May 31 first-round win, edging leftist Iván Cepeda Castro (Historic Pact) at 40.5%, with right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella (33.5%) and center-right Paloma Valencia (25.8%) close behind in a fragmented field. Recent AtlasIntel polling (May 2) shows both Espriella and Valencia beating Cepeda in simulated runoffs—48%-42% and 49%-41%—while GAD3 (April 20-22) has Cepeda leading first round at 38% but vulnerable head-to-head (55%-45% vs. Valencia). March congressional elections delivered Petro's bloc the most seats yet a divided Congress, fueling moderate appeal amid economic pressures, security woes, and Petro fatigue; the race stays tight without right-wing consolidation. Debates, endorsements, or scandals could tip balances before early voting ramps up.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 220-239 tweets from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 5 12:00 PM ET to May 12, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters tightly around 160-219 posts for Elon Musk's X activity from May 5-12, with 180-199 (17.5%), 160-179 (15.5%), and 200-219 (15.5%) leading amid close competition, reflecting uncertainty over his posting cadence post-OpenAI trial. His recent volume dipped to 18-20 daily during April 27 testimony days—55 posts May 2-4 alone—versus historical 28-40 per day during high-engagement periods fueled by Tesla updates, SpaceX launches, or political firestorms. The multi-week trial continuation tempers expectations, but viral potential from breaking news or weekend ramp-up could push higher; traders eye trial recesses and social media buzz as swing factors in this skin-in-the-game sentiment.

markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Despite Nicolás Maduro's January 3, 2026, capture by U.S. forces and extradition to face charges, trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 62.9% probability he resumes as Venezuela's leader by December 31, reflecting doubts on the durability of his ouster amid entrenched chavista institutions, military loyalty, and pro-regime narratives framing it as a kidnapping. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez, Maduro's vice president sworn in January 5, commands 23.5% as she consolidates power past the 90-day constitutional limit, evidenced by recent U.S. oil, gas, and mining deals signaling normalization without elections. Opposition leader María Corina Machado trails at 8%, backed by a unified coalition demanding swift presidential vote upon her planned year-end return, while UN assessments highlight persistent repressive state apparatus and stalled democratic transition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward a 30°C high in Beijing on May 5 (31% implied probability), driven by the shift from May 4's cooler northerly flow—yielding just 21-25°C—to persistent southerly winds fostering warmer advection and enhanced diurnal heating under mostly sunny skies. Official China Meteorological Administration outlooks via World Meteorological Centre Beijing project a 28°C peak at the resolving station, Beijing Capital International Airport, while AccuWeather forecasts 30°C (86°F) with hazy sun and afternoon breezes, highlighting model spread in GFS/ECMWF ensembles around 28-31°C. Differentiating factors include low-level humidity potentially capping peaks below 32°C and exact timing of maximum insolation; hourly station observations will clarify as they unfold tomorrow. Historical May highs average 26-27°C, underscoring above-normal potential from current ridge patterns.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party challenger Woo Sang-ho commands trader consensus at 91% implied probability to win the June 3 Gangwon Special Self-Governing Province governor election, driven by consistent opinion polls showing him ahead of incumbent People Power Party Governor Kim Jin-tae by double digits. Late April surveys from MBC Gangwon affiliates (Woo 50.6%-56% vs. Kim 36.4%) and others like Segye Ilbo-Gallup (DPK 48% vs. PPP 37%) reflect stronger Democratic Party support ratings (45-51% vs. 30-37%), bolstered by Woo's early single nomination and endorsement from former Governor Lee Kwang-jae. With about 30 days remaining, his appeal to centrists and even some conservatives in Yeongdong sustains the lead. Realistic challenges include a major scandal, gaffe, or late consolidation of undecided voters favoring the incumbent amid national trends.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Decibel FDV above ___ one day after launch?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Tampa Bay Rays win the 2026 AL East title?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB American League East division.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the team whose listed nickname comes first alphabetically.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to be named the 2026 American League East division champion per the rules of the MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Baseball (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
